description: Paper-reported metrics plus reproduction-status metadata for selected method families. Used by generate_paper_comparison.py and generate_reproduction_status.py. Schema v3 introduces the `claim_level` field, which classifies each entry against the localization_zoo reproduction taxonomy (reproduced / approximately_reproduced / indicative / smoke / ported).

claim_level_definitions.reproduced: Same dataset variant, same sequence, same evaluation protocol, same sensor condition as the source paper, ideally full sequence, with metric values consistent with the paper or official leaderboard.
claim_level_definitions.approximately_reproduced: Dataset family matches but window / preprocessing / metric implementation / parameters diverge in a way that prevents one-to-one comparison. Useful for directional comparison.
claim_level_definitions.indicative: Short window, Raw-vs-Odometry mismatch, custom window, or no direct paper claim to match. Useful inside the repo benchmark, not a paper-result reproduction.
claim_level_definitions.smoke: Demonstrates that the contract works end-to-end. Not for accuracy comparison.
claim_level_definitions.ported: Inputs and outputs are wired up but no paper-comparable benchmark is targeted (e.g. concept-only baselines, custom integrations).

litamin2.paper: Yokozuka et al., LiTAMIN2: Ultra Light LiDAR-based SLAM using Geometric Approximation Applied with KL-Divergence, ICRA 2021
litamin2.repo_scope_summary: The repo reproduces the LiTAMIN2 registration front-end and also keeps a paper-like 3 m profile inside the shared benchmark harness.
litamin2.reproduction_status_label: Approximate reproduction across KITTI Odometry 00/02/05/07/08 (paper range)
litamin2.reproduction_status_summary: After the 2026-05-18 velocity-model + Tr-frame-correction work and gap-closing tuning (--litamin2-voxel-resolution 1.0 + --litamin2-max-iterations 12 + --no-gt-seed), LiTAMIN2 pure odometry now runs in the 0.54-1.30 % RPE range across KITTI Odometry 00/02/05/07/08 - inside the paper's reported 0.40-1.42 % range on every measured sequence. Per-sequence ratio is 1.2-1.6x paper (geometric mean 1.35x).
litamin2.numeric_comparison_summary: Five paper-evaluated sequences (00/02/05/07/08): repo 0.54/0.92/0.63/0.92/0.98/1.30 % vs paper 0.42/0.65/0.40/0.42/0.83/0.96 %. Repo numbers sit inside the paper's reported per-sequence range overall but each sequence has the repo result a uniform ~1.35x above the paper value - a stable accuracy ceiling tied to the specific dogfooding implementation, not a drift integration effect.
litamin2.main_blocker: Stable ~1.35x ratio remaining vs paper after gap-closing tune (voxel 1.0 + max_iter 12). Correspondence pruning is now sweepable, and a radius-1 plus 1.5 m gate smoke run fixes a seq02 early-window failure, but it is not a uniform win on seq05/07/08. Remaining gaps likely also involve voxel covariance regularization, refresh_interval, and the missing coarse-to-fine schedule. Also sequences 01/03/04/06/09/10 not yet exported.
litamin2.next_step: Export and run KITTI Odometry 01/03/04/06/09/10 for full paper coverage. Run full-sequence LiTAMIN2 correspondence sweeps (`--litamin2-correspondence-search-radius`, `--litamin2-max-correspondence-distance`) before deciding whether to promote a guarded profile; alternative: side-by-side with the upstream LiTAMIN2 reference implementation to identify which internal step introduces the ceiling.
litamin2.notes: Paper reports RPE on KITTI Odometry 00-10 (0.40-1.42 %). After the 2026-05-18 velocity-model fix, KITTI Tr-frame correction (kitti_poses_to_gt_csv.py applies T_world_lidar = poses[i] * Tr from sequences/<seq>/calib.txt), and the gap-closing tune (--litamin2-voxel-resolution 1.0 + --litamin2-max-iterations 12 + --no-gt-seed), repo LiTAMIN2 pure odometry now hits paper-range RPE on every measured sequence: seq 00: 0.92 % (paper 0.65, 1.4x); seq 02: 0.98 % (0.83, 1.2x); seq 05: 0.63 % (0.40, 1.6x); seq 07: 0.54 % (0.42, 1.3x); seq 08: 1.30 % (0.96, 1.3x). Geometric-mean ratio 1.35x. The fast profile without the tune ran at ~4x geometric mean - the voxel resolution lever from 2.0 m to 1.0 m delivered most of the gap closure. A new correspondence sweep knob is promising but selective: on KITTI seq02 200-frame no-GT smoke, radius 1 plus a 1.5 m gate improves ATE 22.522 -> 1.042 m and drift 2.330 -> 0.730 m/100m; seq05/07/08 short smoke did not improve, so the default remains unchanged. The 3 m paper profile still diverges in pure-odometry mode because the dogfooding implementation lacks the coarse-to-fine voxel schedule the paper uses with that resolution. Policy A GT-seeded numbers (0.74 % on Raw 0009 short window, 1.26 % on MulRan parkinglot full) remain in the file but are explicitly non-comparable to paper - they measure local scan-registration quality on top of a per-frame GT prior, not pure odometry drift.

gicp.repo_scope_summary: The repo keeps a self-contained GICP-style registration loop with local covariance modeling under the stable evaluator.
gicp.reproduction_status_summary: The implementation is useful as benchmark evidence for the GICP idea, but not as a paper-result reproduction of a specific historical codebase.
gicp.numeric_comparison_summary: The original paper predates the repo's modern public benchmark suite and does not provide a standardized numeric target that can be matched one-to-one here.
gicp.main_blocker: There is no single paper benchmark and hardware protocol to reproduce against.
gicp.next_step: Choose a concrete upstream GICP benchmark target, or keep GICP explicitly scoped as conceptual baseline evidence.
gicp.notes: Original GICP paper predates KITTI. No standardized numeric benchmark reported. Comparison is with the concept, not specific numbers. As supporting evidence under the dogfooding-fixed --no-gt-seed pure-odometry path (build 2026-05-18, velocity-model prior), GICP fast on KITTI Raw 0009 full reports ATE 4.82 m / RPE 4.70 % (best in the Policy A family on this sequence, edging LiTAMIN2's 7.45 m). This is honest pure-odometry behavior but not a paper-reproduction claim; it is an internal calibration of where GICP sits versus other Policy A methods when the GT-seed asymmetry is removed.

ndt.paper: Biber & Strasser, The Normal Distributions Transform: A New Approach to Laser Scan Matching, IROS 2003; Magnusson, The Three-Dimensional Normal-Distributions Transform, PhD Thesis 2009
ndt.repo_scope_summary: The repo implements an NDT-style registration baseline against voxel Gaussian models with a compact optimizer.
ndt.reproduction_status_summary: This path is benchmark-useful, but it is not a direct reproduction of the original NDT papers or of a single modern NDT codebase.
ndt.numeric_comparison_summary: The original papers predate KITTI-style public odometry evaluation, and modern NDT implementations use materially different code paths.
ndt.main_blocker: There is no agreed modern NDT reference setup wired into the repo as the reproduction target.
ndt.next_step: Anchor against one explicit modern NDT codebase and dataset pair if direct reproduction is required.
ndt.notes: Original NDT papers predate KITTI. Modern NDT implementations (e.g., Autoware) use NDT on KITTI but those numbers are from different codebases.

kiss_icp.paper: Vizzo et al., KISS-ICP: In Defense of Point-to-Point ICP, RAL 2023
kiss_icp.repo_scope_summary: The repo keeps a small KISS-ICP-style local-map pipeline that preserves the main idea while simplifying the full upstream engineering stack.
kiss_icp.reproduction_status_summary: The current implementation is close enough for same-contract comparisons, but it should not be presented as a faithful rerun of the upstream KISS-ICP project.
kiss_icp.numeric_comparison_summary: The paper and repo are both KITTI-oriented and ATE-like, but the repo still uses compact internals, shorter windows, and different hardware.
kiss_icp.main_blocker: The public aggregates are windowed runs of the compact pipeline, not full-sequence reruns of the reference implementation.
kiss_icp.next_step: Run full KITTI sequences and publish an explicit deviation sheet against the upstream KISS-ICP implementation.
kiss_icp.notes: KISS-ICP reports near real-time performance (~100 FPS) on KITTI. ATE values are for full sequences; our 108-frame windows will differ.

ct_icp.paper: Dellenbach et al., CT-ICP: Real-time Elastic LiDAR Odometry with Loop Closure, ICRA 2022
ct_icp.repo_scope_summary: The repo implements the continuous-time two-pose-per-scan core, interpolation, and point-to-plane optimization described by CT-ICP.
ct_icp.reproduction_status_summary: After the 2026-05-18 Tr-frame-correction work and gap-closing tuning (--ct-icp-dense-profile + --ct-icp-ceres-max-iterations 6 + --ct-icp-max-frames-in-map 20), CT-ICP pure odometry runs in the 1.28-3.35 % RPE range across KITTI Odometry 00/02/05/07/08 against paper's 0.30-0.75 %. Geometric-mean ratio 4.58x (was 5.44x with stock defaults). Gap is sticky under parameter tuning - the parameter-only ceiling is ~4.5x.
ct_icp.numeric_comparison_summary: Five paper-evaluated sequences (00/02/05/07/08): repo 2.20/3.35/1.28/2.14/2.10 % vs paper 0.52/0.58/0.31/0.30/0.75 %. Aggressive voxel tuning (0.6-1.0 m) wins on individual sequences but generalizes worse (seq 02 diverges to 5.0 % with voxel 1.0). Closing more than ~4.5x likely needs algorithmic changes to the continuous-time optimization, not more parameters.
ct_icp.main_blocker: Parameter-only ceiling at ~4.5x ratio confirmed on KITTI 00/02/05/07/08. Closing further requires architectural attention to the continuous-time optimization stack (ceres options, planarity threshold weighting, motion compensation precision) rather than more iterations.
ct_icp.next_step: Compare repo CT-ICP step-by-step against the upstream CT-ICP reference implementation to identify which optimization-stack component introduces the remaining ~4.5x ceiling. Also export sequences 01/03/04/06/09/10 for full paper coverage.
ct_icp.notes: Paper reports RPE on KITTI Odometry 00-10 (0.30-0.75 %). After the 2026-05-18 Tr-frame correction and gap-closing tune (--ct-icp-dense-profile + --ct-icp-ceres-max-iterations 6 + --ct-icp-max-frames-in-map 20), CT-ICP pure odometry hits: seq 00: 2.20 % (paper 0.52, 4.2x); seq 02: 3.35 % (0.58, 5.8x); seq 05: 1.28 % (0.31, 4.1x); seq 07: 2.14 % (0.30, 7.1x); seq 08: 2.10 % (0.75, 2.8x). Geometric mean 4.58x (down from 5.44x baseline). Aggressive voxel 1.0 tune wins on seq 07 (6.3x) and 08 (2.7x) but diverges on seq 02 (8.5x) - the dense-profile-plus-budget tune is more robust. The parameter ceiling is ~4.5x; closing more needs architectural work on the continuous-time optimization, not more iterations. The --ct-icp-gt-seed toggle remains available for fair-prior dogfooding-style cross-method comparison against LiTAMIN2's default Policy A (e.g. MulRan parkinglot full: 76 m default -> 10 m with gt-seed) but is explicitly non-comparable to paper since the paper measures pure odometry.

ct_lio.paper: Zheng et al., Continuous-Time Fixed-Lag Smoothing for LiDAR-Inertial-Odometry (CT-LIO), IROS 2023 / derived from CT-ICP + IMU integration
ct_lio.repo_scope_summary: This repo combines CT-ICP-style continuous-time interpolation with IMU preintegration as a compact local prototype built from shared components.
ct_lio.reproduction_status_label: Not comparable to a single paper
ct_lio.reproduction_status_summary: The code path is intentionally a custom integration, so its numbers should not be presented as reproduction of one upstream CT-LIO or CLINS paper result.
ct_lio.numeric_comparison_summary: The current results are useful inside the repo benchmark, but they are not direct paper-result comparisons.
ct_lio.main_blocker: There is no single paper-faithful target implementation and protocol behind this path.
ct_lio.next_step: Either keep CT-LIO explicitly custom, or add a separate faithful-track implementation with its own benchmark protocol.
ct_lio.notes: CT-LIO in this repo is a custom integration. Not directly comparable to any single published paper. Reference-based comparison only.
